title: podman run fails because of overlayfs mount errors
body:
### Issue Description

Using podman to run a postgresql server, the computer was under heavy load then the postgresql container crashed.

I tried restarting the postgresql container but I cannot:

```
+ podman run -d --name xxxxx-xxxx-postgres --rm --net host --shm-size=1g -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=xxxxx -e POSTGRES_USER=xxxx -v /home/mildred/Work/xxxxx/lcas/pgdata-14-lcas:/var/lib/postgresql/data:z postgres:14-alpine postgres -c log_min_duration_statement=250ms
ERRO[0001] Unmounting /home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ddf9ffbeabb280db5c69313cf2d85a74584d66b3c4e75329e4143e21d53e9ff6/merged: invalid argument 
Error: mounting storage for container ae0db0e357cea96dd57ce710942bebf6a2ddc8249568634590747621ba421c2c: creating overlay mount to /home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ddf9ffbeabb280db5c69313cf2d85a74584d66b3c4e75329e4143e21d53e9ff6/merged, mount_data="lowerdir=/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZHT63IMO6EKTUDXDYR2ABXYOUR:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/NCVPHPN2IPF4WEOCIWSQH4DOQ2:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/SU2LFVSWIKONODLI2ZB4DE7INQ:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/MFRYX73LDIWDUTIYWELEYAS2JA:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/43FVFGYCZQUA6IEEPD4BMDW4GY:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/6JALZ6IK3VM43SUNAISCKZF6QY:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/FDYHZ3YNEJKHED4QU7MKKMV6HV:/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/Y7FZOTORU4JDOYPL52PU2ILLXJ,upperdir=/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ddf9ffbeabb280db5c69313cf2d85a74584d66b3c4e75329e4143e21d53e9ff6/diff,workdir=/home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ddf9ffbeabb280db5c69313cf2d85a74584d66b3c4e75329e4143e21d53e9ff6/work,userxattr,volatile,context=\"system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c694,c815\"": input/output error
```

At the same time, I see a kernel error in the system logs:

```
mars 14 17:43:51 faile podman[119152]: 2024-03-14 17:43:51.663532385 +0100 CET m=+1.120398185 image pull 87a6bfe44358e5206672bb1ce973d40754c677bb4398a06654d37bc4329d1c26 postgres:14-alpine
mars 14 17:43:51 faile podman[119152]: 2024-03-14 17:43:51.803662104 +0100 CET m=+1.260527810 container create ae0db0e357cea96dd57ce710942bebf6a2ddc8249568634590747621ba421c2c (image=docker.io/library/postgres:14-alpine, name=codde-lcas-postgres)
mars 14 17:43:51 faile kernel: overlayfs: Cannot mount volatile when upperdir has an unseen error. Sync upperdir fs to clear state.
mars 14 17:43:52 faile podman[119152]: 2024-03-14 17:43:52.054193101 +0100 CET m=+1.511058807 container remove ae0db0e357cea96dd57ce710942bebf6a2ddc8249568634590747621ba421c2c (image=docker.io/library/postgres:14-alpine, name=xxxxx-xxxx-postgres)
```

In partisular this kernel error looks suspect: **overlayfs: Cannot mount volatile when upperdir has an unseen error. Sync upperdir fs to clear state.**

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start the container
2. Hope for a crash that will leave the container in a bad state
3. Try to restart the container


### Describe the results you received

Podman failed to recover and restart the container

### Describe the results you expected

Podman shsould recover the bad state and start the container

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The container was removed prior to restarting it, any rootless `podman run` for the same user is failing. `podman run` as root or as other users continue to work.

comments.body: Usually a computer restart can fix this issue.

you can't restart a container that was created with `--rm`.  That flag means the container must be deleted once it terminates.

Does podman show any error if it is left in a bad state and you try removing it?

Sorry, I was not explaining correctly. I am not restarting the container. It's just that I have a script that's called postgresql-restart that does a `podman stop`/`podman rm` (in case of leftover container) followed by a `podman run`.

The postgresql container seems gone when the problem occurs (not shown in `podman ps`) but it seems that the overlayfs storage directory is not clean because if I try to run *any* container when in this bad state I get the error:

```
ERRO[0000] Unmounting /home/mildred/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/223653d7679006f3de3df4b0bd529b7712c8df798b3fdc64678bc8cd74266818/merged: invalid argument
Error: mounting storage for container 50a9c2b213a21405ccaeab9cec9e30e5cc9aa152404d2e1926bd0f8ec997cbfc: creating overlay mount to ...: input/output error
```

My only solution is to restart the computer (or run my containers as another user)

Probably `podman system reset` would solve the issue too but I have toolbox containers that I'd like to keep.
can you share the output of `podman unshare cat /proc/self/mountinfo` when you get into this state?

I think I am encountering the same bug on Fedora 39, below my output.
I cannot pinpoint exactly when it occurs. I mostly run individual build commands using `podman run -it --rm --init` and sometimes cancel them using Ctrl+C.

```

I remember trying that but it did not help, but I am not 100% sure. Will try once more once it occurs again to be 100% sure (or someone else does it before me).
Not an FS expert, but I would also be surprised if sync really meant sync here, because if so, I would expect the kernel to do the sync automatically.

I am not sure either, but from the code above it seems it is checking the upper layer superblock.

I think this behavior is a consequence of this change https://github.com/torvalds/linux/commit/335d3fc57941e5c6164c69d439aec1cb7a800876
@sargun any hint what it could cause this issue?  Is it a pending error on the upper layer file system that was not checked yet?
This can happen if your upperdir has had filesystem errors. They _should_ get cleared if you do sync (upperdir). If that doesn't fix it, can you give me a drgn dumb of the upperdir sb?

I have run `sync` and it did not fix the issue. Fortunately, however, I am going on vacation, so I can leave my machine in this state to investigate the issue further on Monday when I am back. So if anyone has input on what to try, what information to collect, shoot ;-)

This just happened on my silverblue host (kernel-6.8.6-200.fc39.x86_64, podman-4.9.4-1.fc39.x86_64 and fuse-overlayfs-1.13-1.fc39.x86_64). I was running a heavy i/o workload on a bind mounted host volume, and it appears like this resulted in file corruptions:

```
Apr 21 13:50:28 fedora audit[2050323]: ANOM_ABEND auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0 pid=2050323 comm="cat" exe="/nix/store/asqa3kfq3maclk7cqqhrjvp7vriw6ahy-coreutils-9.5/bin/coreutils" sig=11 res=1

Apr 21 13:51:55 fedora systemd[2112]: Started app-gnome-podenv-2050882.scope - Application launched by gnome-shell.
Apr 21 13:51:56 fedora gnome-shell[2050928]: time="2024-04-21T13:51:56-04:00" level=warning msg="The input device is not a TTY. The --tty and --interactive flags might not work properly"
Apr 21 13:51:56 fedora podman[2050928]: 2024-04-21 13:51:56.617979904 -0400 EDT m=+0.074476530 container create 58f7b663c0ab2730a60bb96d6e74696e54ebfb861134bea6b83f49b16f289471 (image=localhost/silverkube4:latest, name=host-nmacs, vendor=Fedora Project, version=38, io.buildah.version=1.33.2, >
Apr 21 13:51:56 fedora kernel: overlayfs: Cannot mount volatile when upperdir has an unseen error. Sync upperdir fs to clear state.
Apr 21 13:51:56 fedora gnome-shell[2050928]: time="2024-04-21T13:51:56-04:00" level=error msg="Unmounting /var/home/tristanc/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f8957134448bf64f5a41341fcbaeb7eb8a8e3a693f140916d4f5ac7fe21cedd8/merged: invalid argument"
```

At that point, starting container always failed with that overlayfs error. Rebooting and removing the affected volume fixed the issue.
Can you dump the superblock of the upperdir using drgn?
Let me see if it still exists. What is drgn?

Got this issue again, I was under the impression that to sync the filesystems, you just had to call `sync` but I read the manpage again and I did a `sync -f ~` to sync my homedir (could not sync the upperdir because it does not exists after podman errors out.

I did it twice, getting an IO error the first time (in French, sorry) and not the second time. And hooray, podman works again

```
